title: Model-Native Arcade
summary: A staged redesign path for keeping GVGAI/VGDL as the source of game truth while moving selected games toward a lightweight non-Java runtime and game-specific LoRA adapters served by Legion vLLM.

games.name: aliens
games.logicShape: Lane shooter with enemy descent, missile collision, and clear-all termination.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, avatar lane, alien rows, missile state, score, tick, and ACTION_LEFT/ACTION_RIGHT/ACTION_USE/ACTION_NIL.
games.finetuneData: Human and scripted traces that show lane alignment, dodge timing, and fire cadence.
games.featuredCopy: Hold the firing lane while a tuned model learns when to shoot and when to dodge.

games.name: boulderchase
games.logicShape: Collector loop with moving threats, resource pickup, and obstacle-aware pathing.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, avatar position, resource cells, chaser cells, blocked cells, score, tick, and movement actions.
games.finetuneData: Runs that trade off safe collection routes against enemy distance and score pressure.
games.featuredCopy: Collect under pressure while the adapter learns safe greed.

games.name: cakybaky
games.logicShape: Timed collection game with hazards, resource count, and compact grid navigation.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, target count, hazard offsets, timeout pressure, score, tick, and movement actions.
games.finetuneData: Short traces that cover fast collection, hazard avoidance, and timeout recovery.
games.featuredCopy: A small timed board for measuring whether fine-tuning improves urgency.

games.name: chase
games.logicShape: Reactive collection with state changes and enemy pressure.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, avatar position, collectible positions, enemy positions, transformed states, score, tick, and movement actions.
games.finetuneData: Contrasting defensive and scoring traces that expose when state changes matter.
games.featuredCopy: Route through a changing board while the model balances score and survival.

games.name: butterflies
games.logicShape: Deliberate chase game with target pursuit and clear-all objectives.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, avatar position, target positions, blocked cells, score, tick, and movement actions.
games.finetuneData: Target-order traces that demonstrate pursuit planning instead of random wandering.
games.featuredCopy: A clean chase cabinet for testing whether the adapter can plan a target order.

games.name: chipschallenge
games.logicShape: Puzzle navigation with resource gates, movable blockers, and goal progress.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, inventory/resource state, blockers, gates, avatar position, score, tick, and movement actions.
games.finetuneData: Solved-path traces with intermediate inventory states and wrong-turn recovery.
games.featuredCopy: A deliberate puzzle board for testing memory over visible rules.

games.name: chopper
games.logicShape: Roaming shooter with hazards, attack timing, and navigation around threat lanes.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, avatar position, enemy positions, projectile or use-action availability, score, tick, and movement/use actions.
games.finetuneData: Traces that pair pursuit, retreat, and use-action timing under threat.
games.featuredCopy: Roam, line up, and fire without losing the board state.

games.name: clusters
games.logicShape: Reactive pusher puzzle with clustered objects and timeout pressure.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, object clusters, avatar position, blocked cells, timeout state, score, tick, and movement actions.
games.finetuneData: Traces that show object approach order, cluster separation, and timeout-aware moves.
games.featuredCopy: A pressure puzzle for measuring whether the model can keep object goals straight.

games.name: digdug
games.logicShape: Digging and navigation game with enemy avoidance and attack opportunities.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, diggable cells, avatar position, enemy offsets, use-action label, score, tick, and movement/use actions.
games.finetuneData: Traces that distinguish digging routes, safe tunnels, and attack timing.
games.featuredCopy: A digger cabinet for exposing spatial planning in the prompt contract.

games.name: pacman
games.logicShape: Maze pursuit with pellets, threats, transformation windows, and route choice.
games.contract: Grid snapshot, avatar position, pellet/resource cells, enemy positions, power state, score, tick, and movement actions.
games.finetuneData: Traces that cover pellet routing, ghost distance, and power-state reversals.
games.featuredCopy: Classic maze pressure for comparing base prompts against a tuned game adapter.

phases.title: Inventory the portable rules
phases.description: Parse the selected VGDL and level files into a compact manifest: sprites, interactions, terminations, controls, level maps, and known unsupported ontology calls.

phases.title: Build the no-Java subset
phases.description: Implement only the gridphysics ontology needed by the ten starter games, then keep Java as the oracle until JS state transitions match sampled ticks.

phases.title: Stabilize model I/O
phases.description: Make each game expose a deterministic observation summary and canonical ACTION_* output contract that works for base models and tuned adapters.

phases.title: Train and serve adapters
phases.description: Convert traces into chat JSONL, train QLoRA adapters with stable IDs, serve them through Legion vLLM, and register them beside baseline models.

phases.title: Promote by evidence
phases.description: Use eval survival, score, action diversity, and prompt sensitivity to decide when a migrated game becomes a featured model-native cabinet.

sourceReferences.name: Local GVGAI VGDL files
sourceReferences.role: Canonical source for the ten starter games and their levels.

sourceReferences.name: PyVGDL
sourceReferences.role: Reference implementation for VGDL parsing and ontology separation.

sourceReferences.name: UnityVGDL
sourceReferences.role: Reference for translating VGDL ontology concepts into a non-Java engine.

sourceReferences.name: GVGAI_GYM
sourceReferences.role: Reference for treating GVGAI games as agent-facing environments.

sourceReferences.name: AI Safety Gridworlds
sourceReferences.role: Reference for small gridworld environment structure and evaluation discipline.
